items.description: Public struct/type `Greeter` with `greet(name: String) -> String` returning "Hello, <name>!" and `greet() -> String` returning "Hello, world!"

rules_checked.reasoning: The item is declared kind 'type' and describes a public struct/type Greeter with plain value-returning methods (greet, greet(name:)). This is exactly a type declaration with no service orchestration, no protocol, no view — 'type' is the correct kind for what it actually is.

Package.swift defines a single target named 'Greeter' with sources expected under Sources/Greeter. The target path Sources/Greeter/Greeter.swift is the canonical home for the Greeter type in this target, and it is inside declared_scope. Correct module home.

This is a trivial single-package walking-skeleton project with no view layer, view-model layer, or data-access layer present. The greet logic is plain string formatting with no data access or view concerns, so there is no domain logic being placed above its correct altitude — the rule is satisfied vacuously/correctly since there is only one layer (Service-equivalent) in this project.

Sources/Greeter/Greeter.swift does not yet exist (Sources/Greeter/ is currently empty per directory listing) and there is no existing type to extend. Introducing this one new type for the entire feature's public API is right-sized — a single struct with two closely related greet methods, not split into multiple types nor merged into something unrelated. SRP respected.

Greeter is a noun naming a type per Swift API Design Guidelines; greet(name:) and greet() are verb methods with a clear argument label for the value being greeted, following Swift naming conventions (no Hungarian notation, no redundant type name in method names).

The item introduces a concrete struct with no protocol/abstraction at all. No speculative protocol is being introduced, so there is nothing to substitute or seam, and none is proposed — compliant by not over-abstracting.

Greeter.swift is a leaf type with no described imports or dependencies on other modules; Package.swift shows the Greeter target has no external dependencies, so no forbidden import or reversed dependency direction is smuggled in.
